commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 5. "This man shall receive blessing from the Lord, and mercy from the God of his salvation" (ver. 5).

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh He shall receive the blessing from the LORD, and righteousness from the God of his salvation. David seems to have given the eulogium of Obededom, whose example taught him that the ark was only terrible to the wicked; and that it was a source of blessings to the just, 2 Kings vi. Hebrew, "justice. "But these terms are used synonymously, and denote that God gives a just reward; "when he crowns our merits, he crowns his own gifts. Augustine; Theodoret) (Calmet) Mercy goes before; good works must follow, to obtain eternal glory. (Worthington).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
